skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Complete code review workflow for both requesting and receiving reviews. Use when creating PRs, reviewing code, or responding to feedback.
skill.summary: Guide AI-assisted pull request preparation, review responses, and review checklists.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported shell backtick execution and weak cryptography, but the cited lines are Markdown front matter and fenced templates, not executable code. No malicious intent, exfiltration pattern, prompt injection, or actual command execution was found; the only residual concern is broad declared tool access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Treated as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 26 opens a fenced Markdown PR template. It is documentation text, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line starts a Markdown code fence labeled markdown. The surrounding lines contain a pull request description template, with no executable command or interpolation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Closing Markdown Fence Treated as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 42 closes the PR template code fence. It does not execute shell, Ruby, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is only three backticks closing a documentation block. There is no command string, interpreter invocation, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Response Template Fence Treated as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 59 opens a fenced Markdown block for reviewer response examples. The content is prose for communication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited block contains suggested review response phrases. It has no shell syntax, subprocess invocation, or instruction to run external commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 3 is a plain description of the skill workflow. No weak cryptographic algorithm is named or used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains only natural-language metadata about code review. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other cryptographic usage in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Broad Tool Permissions Declared
low_findings.description: The skill declares Read, Glob, Grep, Edit, Write, and Bash access in its front matter. This is not malicious by itself, but marketplace users should understand that agents may use filesystem and shell tools while following the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission list is directly visible in the front matter. The concern is limited because the skill does not include specific commands, network access, credential handling, or unsafe execution instructions.

content.value_statement: Code reviews often lose time because pull requests lack context or feedback responses are unclear. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for PR descriptions, review readiness, and reviewer replies.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured pull request descriptions with What, Why, How, Testing, and Screenshots sections.
Checks review readiness with testing, linting, scope, and commit message reminders.
Guides response language for accepted feedback, design explanations, and respectful disagreement.
Separates reviewer and author practices for clear collaboration during code review.
Encourages small change sets, self-review, and priority labels for feedback.
limitations: Does not run tests or lint commands by itself unless the agent chooses separate tool actions.
Does not inspect repository-specific review rules beyond information available in the workspace.
Does not replace human judgment for architecture, security, or product decisions.
Contains Korean guidance, so teams may need translated wording for English-only workflows.

use_cases.title: Draft a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Create a concise PR description that explains the change, reason, implementation, and testing status.

use_cases.title: Respond to Review Feedback
use_cases.description: Prepare clear replies for accepted changes, design questions, and respectful disagreement.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Reviews
use_cases.description: Use consistent reviewer expectations for priority labels, constructive comments, and final approval checks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a PR Description
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review skill to draft a PR description for my current changes. Include What, Why, How, Testing, and Screenshots sections.

prompt_templates.title: Check Review Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review checklist to review my branch before I open a PR. Highlight missing tests, unclear scope, and commit message issues.

prompt_templates.title: Reply to Reviewer Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review response patterns to help me answer these reviewer comments. Separate accepted changes, explanations, and disagreements.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Team Review Standard
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review skill to create a short team review guide. Include expectations for PR size, context, feedback priority, and approval readiness.

output_examples.input: Draft a PR description for a login validation fix.
output_examples.output: A structured PR description with a short change summary, reason for the fix, implementation notes, testing checklist, and screenshot placeholder.

output_examples.input: Help me answer a reviewer who asked why I kept this service method.
output_examples.output: A respectful response that explains the design reason, invites alternatives, and identifies any follow-up changes.

output_examples.input: Check whether this PR is ready for review.
output: Flags unclear scope or missing context.
Suggests a self-review before requesting approval.

best_practices: Provide the diff, test results, and target audience before asking for review help.
Keep pull requests small enough that reviewers can understand the change quickly.
Ask the agent to separate must-fix issues from suggestions and discussion points.
anti_patterns: Do not use generated review responses without checking technical accuracy.
Do not ask the skill to hide missing tests or unresolved reviewer concerns.
Do not treat the checklist as a substitute for project-specific release rules.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps prepare pull request descriptions, review checklists, and clear responses to reviewer feedback.

faq.question: Can it review code quality directly?
faq.answer: It can guide review structure and identify likely gaps when given code context, but it is mainly a workflow skill.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It reminds the agent to check tests, but command execution depends on the active tool environment.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill include security review guidance?
faq.answer: Only general code review workflow guidance is included. Use a dedicated security review process for high-risk changes.

faq.question: What language is the source guidance written in?
faq.answer: Most workflow guidance is written in Korean, with standard pull request section names in English.
